symptoms.symptom_name: Acute subluxations that spontaneously reduced, with reactive muscle spasm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent subluxations reducing spontaneously with reactive spasm on a hypermobility background is the hEDS musculoskeletal phenotype.
Normal joint contour with no stuck-out deformity argues against a persistent dislocation needing reduction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact distal neurovascular status (warm, normal colour, normal sensation/movement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm, normally-perfused limb with intact sensation/movement argues against neurovascular compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever or single hot/red joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever and mechanically-linked multi-joint pain argue against a septic joint.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stuck on floor/couch, unable to transfer/reach water, isolated (fall/self-care risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immobility with inability to transfer/reach fluids while alone is a genuine welfare risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shoulder located/no deformity; patella tracking with tender medial retinaculum/mild effusion; intact distal neurovascular status; Beighton 8/9 (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Located joints with reactive spasm/mild effusion and a high Beighton score fit the hEDS phenotype.
Stable ligament tests argue against an acute rupture, though chronic laxity/occult tears may need imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known hEDS with recurrent instability/early OA

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: hEDS is the substrate for recurrent subluxations and reactive spasm.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: hEDS joint instability flare (reduced subluxations with reactive spasm)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Subluxations that have spontaneously reduced, with no stuck-out deformity and intact distal neurovascular status, are an hEDS instability flare — not a frank dislocation or neurovascular emergency. Management is analgesia, joint support/bracing, physiotherapy and fall-safety, with urgent in-person review (± imaging for occult tears) — but a joint stuck out or a cold/numb limb means ED/000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person joint and neurovascular examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm reduction, assess stability/effusion and neurovascular status; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Imaging (X-ray/MRI) if a persistent dislocation, fracture or occult labral/meniscal tear is suspected

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For joint stabilisation and recurrence prevention.
